study_info.title: The rare intracellular RET mutation p.S891A in a Chinese Han family with familial medullary thyroid carcinoma
study_info.study_type: Familial case series with genetic analysis
institutions: Department of Oncologic and Urologic Surgery, the 117th PLA Hospital, Anhui Medical University, 40 Jichang Road, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, 310004, China
Department of Neck and Thoracic Oncologic Surgery, Anhui Provincial Cancer Hospital, Anhui Medical University, No. 107 Huan-hu East Road, Hefei 230031, China
study_info.funding: Key Scientific Research Project of Nanjing Military Command, China (09Z038 and 10Z036).
study_info.ethics_approval: Ethics Committee of the 117th PLA Hospital (study protocol approved; written informed consent obtained from all patients and/or legal guardians).

cohort_info.pedigree_description: Four-generation southern Chinese Han pedigree with familial medullary thyroid carcinoma (FMTC) from Zhejiang Province, China.
cohort_info.MEN2_subtype_diagnosis_criteria: FMTC diagnosed in families with four or more cases of MTC in the absence of pheochromocytoma or hyperparathyroidism, according to International Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia Workshop criteria.

MEN2_related_manifestations_in_family.PHEO: Absent in all six affected individuals.
MEN2_related_manifestations_in_family.HPT: Absent in all six affected individuals.
MEN2_related_manifestations_in_family.HD: Absent in all six affected individuals.
MEN2_related_manifestations_in_family.CNT: Absent in all six affected individuals.

cohort_info.follow_up_policy: For MTC: calcitonin and plasma calcium every 6 months, with annual detailed imaging if postoperative calcitonin is abnormal. For RET mutation carriers older than 20 years: annual biochemical and imaging examinations to screen for pheochromocytoma and hyperparathyroidism, with lifetime follow-up.
cohort_info.notes: All 14 family members underwent Doppler ultrasound and serum calcitonin measurement; medical history and physical examination were also obtained.

reported_mutations.domain_affected: intracellular tyrosine kinase 2 (TK2) region of RET protein
reported_mutations.clinical_classification: MEN2-related; categorized as ATA-A level mutation (lower transforming activity, milder disease) per 2009 ATA guidelines.
databases_checked: MEN2 mutation database (mentioned in context for other variants)

frequency_in_other_populations.summarized_carriers_prior_literature: 85 carriers with p.S891A reported; medullary thyroid carcinoma in 72.9% (62/85), pheochromocytoma in 3.5% (3/85), hyperparathyroidism in 3.5% (3/85) and corneal nerve thickening in 3.5% (3/85); mean age of MTC diagnosis 42.3 years.

reported_mutations.clinical_classification: Polymorphism; reported as associated with disease susceptibility in some populations but no association in this family.
reported_mutations.frequency_in_family: Present in multiple members as shown in Table 1.

reported_mutations.clinical_classification: Variant; not previously reported to play a role in MEN2-related medullary thyroid carcinoma; suggested to be benign or a SNP and possibly an inhibitory modifier.
reported_mutations.presence_in_controls: Listed as rs35118262 in dbSNP/Ensembl; no disease association in MEN2 database.
reported_mutations.frequency_in_family: Identified in III-6 (with p.S891A) and in unaffected IV-1 and IV-3.

reported_mutations.clinical_classification: Polymorphism; associated with disease susceptibility in some populations but no association with phenotype in this family.
reported_mutations.frequency_in_family: Present in several members per Table 1.

reported_mutations.clinical_classification: Polymorphism; reported in several thyroid and Hirschsprung disease studies but no phenotype association here.
Variant/SNP; implicated as a disease modifier in some populations, but no association in this family.
reported_mutations.frequency_in_family: Present in some carriers including IV-2 (Table 1).

reported_mutations.clinical_classification: Polymorphism; reported as a disease modifier in some cohorts but no association here.
reported_mutations.frequency_in_family: Present in several members per Table 1.

patients.family_history: Member of FMTC pedigree with multiple affected relatives.
patients.presenting_symptoms: Identified by family screening and RET testing; no specific presenting symptoms described.

imaging.neck_ultrasound: Right biggest lobe multi-centric nodule 0.9 × 0.7 × 0.5 cm; left biggest lobe multi-centric nodule 0.8 × 0.7 × 0.5 cm; bilateral neck lymph node metastases.
imaging.CT: Performed; specific findings beyond neck nodules not detailed.

pathology.lymph_nodes: 17 positive lymph nodes out of 60 resected.

surgeries.type: Total thyroidectomy with modified bilateral neck dissection
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: After identification of elevated preoperative calcitonin and RET mutation (November 2012).

lymph_nodes.compartments_dissected: Bilateral neck compartments; level VI not separately specified.

patients.follow_up_status: No recurrence on biochemical (post-Ct 2.92 ng/L) and imaging examinations.

patients.presenting_symptoms: Palpable neck mass in right thyroid in 1999.

imaging.US_1999: Hypoechoic nodules 3.3 × 2.8 × 2.2 cm with calcifications in right thyroid lobe and right neck mass.
imaging.US_2013: Multi-centric hypoechoic nodules in left thyroid lobe (maximum 1.5 × 1.5 × 0.7 cm) and left neck mass.
imaging.CT: Used in workup; details not separately stated.

pathology.thyroid: 1999: right lobe medullary thyroid carcinoma; 2013: bilateral multi-centric MTC.
pathology.lymph_nodes: Bilateral neck lymph node metastases at final surgery; numbers not available.

surgeries.type: Total right thyroidectomy and modified right neck dissection
surgeries.comments: Initial treatment without awareness of FMTC; considered non-normative in retrospect.

surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: Second and third surgeries in 2012 for multiple right neck masses and elevated biochemical markers.

surgeries.type: Total left thyroidectomy and modified left neck dissection
surgeries.extent: Completion thyroidectomy and left neck dissection.
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: Fourth surgery in April 2013 after detection of left-sided disease and Ct 688.30 ng/L.
surgeries.comments: Histopathology revealed bilateral multi-centric MTC with bilateral lymph node metastases.

lymph_nodes.compartments_dissected: Right and left neck compartments over multiple surgeries.

patients.follow_up_status: Postoperative calcitonin remained elevated at 358.20 ng/L, indicating persistent biochemical disease; no specific structural recurrence described beyond neck disease already treated.
patients.outcomes: Alive with persistent biochemical evidence of disease.

patients.family_history: Brother of proband (III-2); father of IV-2; member of FMTC pedigree. Note: II-3 is the proband's uncle (a different branch of the family).
patients.presenting_symptoms: Detected via RET screening and elevated calcitonin rather than specific symptoms.

imaging.US: Right biggest lobe multi-centric nodules 1.8 × 1.5 × 1.0 cm; left biggest lobe multi-centric nodules 2.3 × 1.7 × 1.5 cm; bilateral neck lymph node metastases.
imaging.ECT: Showed seventh posterior rib metastasis 5 months after surgery.
imaging.MRI: Confirmed seventh posterior rib metastasis 5 months after surgery.
imaging.CT: Performed; specific thyroid findings described by ultrasound.

pathology.other_sites: Seventh posterior rib metastasis (bone) detected postoperatively.

surgeries.type: Total thyroidectomy with modified bilateral neck dissection
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: After family investigation and RET scanning in November 2012.
surgeries.comments: Normative surgery but disease already advanced.

staging.notes: T2N1bM1 due to seventh posterior rib metastasis.

patients.follow_up_status: Distant posterior rib metastasis appeared 5 months post-surgery; calcitonin and CEA decreased but remained elevated; patient refused further treatment.
patients.outcomes: Alive with metastatic disease; no additional therapy after refusal.

patients.family_history: RET double-mutation carrier (p.S891A/p.T278N) in FMTC pedigree.
patients.outcomes: Alive, no structural MTC; mildly elevated calcitonin only.

patients.presenting_symptoms: Multi-centric hypoechoic nodules and neck mass detected by ultrasound and CT; no prior specific symptoms stated.

imaging.US_2009: Multi-centric hypoechoic nodules (maximum 1.5 × 0.8 × 0.5 cm) in left thyroid lobe and left neck mass.
imaging.CT_2009: Confirmed left multi-centric nodules and left neck mass.
imaging.US_CT_2012: Multi-centric hypoechoic nodules with calcifications in right thyroid lobe and right neck mass.

pathology.thyroid: 2009: left multi-centric MTC; 2012: right lobe MTC; overall bilateral MTC history.

surgeries.type: Total left thyroidectomy with modified left neck dissection
surgeries.extent: Thyroidectomy focused on left lobe; left neck dissection.
surgeries.comments: Histology: left multi-centric MTC with left lymph node metastases.

surgeries.type: Total right thyroidectomy with modified right neck dissection
surgeries.extent: Right lobe thyroidectomy; right neck dissection.
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: Three years after 2009, around 2012, after right-sided nodules and Ct elevation detected.
surgeries.comments: Histology: multi-centric MTC with right lymph node metastases.

lymph_nodes.compartments_dissected: Left and right neck compartments in separate operations.

patients.follow_up_status: Postoperative calcitonin remained mildly elevated at 9.20 ng/L; continued follow-up.
patients.outcomes: Alive with mild biochemical persistence; no reported structural recurrence at the time of publication.

patients.family_history: At-risk family member identified during systematic family RET screening.
patients.presenting_symptoms: Asymptomatic; small thyroid nodule detected on imaging.

pathology.thyroid: Bilateral medullary thyroid carcinoma (Bi-MTC) with small tumor focus (0.5 × 0.5 × 0.4 cm).
pathology.lymph_nodes: 0 positive lymph nodes out of 4 resected in level VI.

surgeries.type: Total thyroidectomy with level VI lymph node dissection
surgeries.extent: Total thyroidectomy; prophylactic level VI compartment dissection.
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: After elevated calcitonin (12.60 ng/L) and detection of RET mutation.
surgeries.comments: Later discussed as potentially unnecessary given low calcitonin.

patients.follow_up_status: No recurrence; postoperative calcitonin <2.00 ng/L; imaging examinations negative.

patients.relationship_in_pedigree: Child in generation IV (specific parent described as III-2 in text).
patients.family_history: At-risk family member; carries p.T278N but not p.S891A.
patients.outcomes: Alive, no evidence of medullary thyroid carcinoma.

patients.relationship_in_pedigree: Child in generation IV (exact parent not clearly specified).
patients.family_history: At-risk family member; carries p.T278N variant.
patients.outcomes: Alive, no evidence of medullary thyroid carcinoma.

case_data.US_results: Right biggest lobe multi-centric 0.9 × 0.7 × 0.5 cm; left biggest lobe multi-centric 0.8 × 0.7 × 0.5 cm; bilateral neck lymph node metastases.
Right lobe 3.3 × 2.8 × 2.2 cm; right neck lymph node metastases.
Left biggest lobe multi-centric 1.5 × 1.5 × 0.7 cm; left neck lymph node metastases.
Right biggest lobe multi-centric 1.8 × 1.5 × 1.0 cm; left biggest lobe multi-centric 2.3 × 1.7 × 1.5 cm; bilateral neck lymph node metastases.
Left biggest lobe multi-centric 1.5 × 0.8 × 0.5 cm; left neck lymph node metastases.
case_data.histology: Left multicentric MTC with left lymph node metastases (Left-MTC at first surgery; overall bilateral MTC after second surgery).

case_data.US_results: Right biggest lobe multi-centric 0.6 × 0.5 × 0.5 cm; right neck lymph node metastases.

p.S891A_family_statistics.MTC_penetrance_in_family: 83.3% (5/6) at reported ages; typically MTC-only phenotype.

p.S891A_clinical_behavior.transforming_activity: Low transforming activity in vitro relative to high-risk RET mutations.
p.S891A_clinical_behavior.biochemical_properties: RET S891A mutant functions as an active monomer; does not require dimerization; ligand binding does not further enhance autophosphorylation or downstream signaling.
p.S891A_clinical_behavior.associated_phenotypes_from_literature: Initially described as FMTC mutation; also linked to MEN2A features (pheochromocytoma, hyperparathyroidism) in some pedigrees.

other_variant_notes.SNPs_clinical_role: These SNPs have been associated with modification of disease susceptibility and clinical phenotypes (C-cell hyperplasia, sporadic MTC, papillary thyroid carcinoma, Hirschsprung disease, MEN2A, FMTC) in some populations, but no phenotype associations were observed in this family.
other_variant_notes.p.T278N_note: p.T278N has not been reported to play a role in MEN2-related MTC; in this family it co-occurs with p.S891A in one mildly affected biochemical carrier and in two unaffected younger carriers, suggesting a benign or possibly inhibitory modifier role, but further studies are needed.

methods.DNA_source: Genomic DNA extracted from EDTA anti-coagulated peripheral blood from all available family members.
methods.sequencing_methods: Entire coding region of RET amplified and sequenced in both sense and antisense directions using ABI Prism 377 automatic sequencer (Perkin-Elmer, USA).
methods.primers: PCR conditions and primers as previously described in Qi et al. 2011 and 2012; not detailed in this article.
methods.mutation_analysis_guidelines: Analyses performed according to 2009 ATA recommendations and 2001 European recommendations.

clinical_investigations.ultrasound: All 14 family members underwent neck Doppler ultrasound.
clinical_investigations.calcitonin_assay: Basal serum calcitonin measured using fully-automated chemiluminescence immunoassay (FACLIA), Immulite 2000 Immunoassay System, Siemens Ltd., Germany.
clinical_investigations.biochemical_evaluation: CEA (normal <5.0 ng/L), serum PTH by electrochemiluminescence, serum catecholamines by radioimmunoassay (RIA), and serum calcium by arsenazo III method.
imaging_modalities: Computed tomography (CT) for RET mutation carriers.
Emission CT (ECT) and MRI when abnormalities were detected.
Adrenal CT for pheochromocytoma screening in selected carriers.
clinical_investigations.tumor_staging: American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC) 7th edition TNM classification.
clinical_investigations.lymph_node_status_definition: Defined according to American Thyroid Association (ATA) recommendations.

follow_up.MTC_follow_up: Calcitonin and plasma calcium every 6 months; annual detailed imaging if postoperative calcitonin is abnormal.
follow_up.RET_mutation_carriers_follow_up: After initial screening in carriers older than 20 years, annual biochemical and imaging examinations for pheochromocytoma and hyperparathyroidism, with lifetime follow-up.
guideline_references: ATA management guidelines for medullary thyroid carcinoma (Kloos et al. 2009).
Previous reports by Qi et al. 2013 on prophylactic thyroidectomy in MEN2-related MTC.

methods.statistical_tests: No formal statistical tests reported; descriptive statistics only.

RET_mutation_detection.p.S891A: Heterozygous c.2671T>G (p.S891A) mutation in exon 15 identified in 6 of 14 family members.
RET_mutation_detection.other_variants: SNPs c.135A>G (p.A45A, exon 2), IVS4+48A>G (intron 4), c.1296A>G (p.A432A, exon 7), c.2071G>A (p.G691S, exon 11), c.2307T>G (p.L769L, exon 13) and variant c.833C>A (p.T278N, exon 4) detected.
RET_mutation_detection.MTC_phenotype: Five of six p.S891A carriers had medullary thyroid carcinoma as an isolated clinical phenotype; one double carrier (p.S891A/p.T278N) remained asymptomatic with slightly elevated calcitonin.
RET_mutation_detection.p.T278N_carriers: Two unaffected members (IV-1, IV-3) with p.T278N had normal findings and consistently undetectable calcitonin levels.

clinical_features.surgeries_performed: All MTC patients underwent total unilateral or bilateral thyroidectomy with unilateral or bilateral neck dissection and/or level VI lymph node dissection.
clinical_features.MEN2_manifestations: None of the six affected individuals (five MTC, one biochemical carrier) had pheochromocytoma, hyperparathyroidism, Hirschsprung disease or corneal nerve thickening.

surgical_outcomes.non_normative_surgeries: Two patients (III-2 and III-10) underwent multiple non-normative thyroid and neck surgeries initially, before recognition of FMTC and RET mutation status, resulting in persistent elevated calcitonin and repeat operations.
surgical_outcomes.normative_surgeries: Patients II-3 and III-3 underwent total thyroidectomy with modified bilateral neck dissection; patient IV-2 underwent total thyroidectomy with prophylactic level VI compartment dissection.
surgical_outcomes.biochemical_remission: Calcitonin normalized postoperatively in patients II-3 (2.92 ng/L) and IV-2 (<2.00 ng/L).
surgical_outcomes.persistent_or_metastatic_disease: Patients III-2 and III-10 had elevated postoperative calcitonin (358.20 and 9.20 ng/L); patient III-3 developed seventh posterior rib metastasis despite surgery, with calcitonin reduced but still elevated (1267.20 ng/L).
surgical_outcomes.asymptomatic_carrier_management: Carrier III-6 with p.S891A/p.T278N and mildly elevated calcitonin declined prophylactic thyroidectomy and was monitored.

functional_data.model_system: No functional experiments performed in this study; functional properties referenced from previous in vitro work.
functional_data.phosphorylation_sites: Not measured in this article; p.S891A known to affect RET autophosphorylation.
functional_data.signaling_pathways_activated: RET familial MTC mutants including S891A can activate Src/JAK/STAT3 pathway independently of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (from Plaza Menacho et al. 2005).
functional_data.assay_types: No in vitro kinase or cell signaling assays were performed in this study.

comparative_strength_vs_known_mutants.comment: p.S891A is grouped with RET mutations showing low transforming activity in vitro.

Table_1.title_or_caption: Sequence variants/polymorphisms of RET found in 14 cases
Table_1.description: Lists each family member (I-2, II-3, III-2, III-3, III-6, III-8, III-10, III-11, IV-1, IV-2, IV-3, IV-4, IV-5, IV-6) with gender and presence (+) of RET variants A45A, T278N, IVS4+48A>G, A432A, G691S, L769L.
Table_1.numeric_values_present: Presence/absence markers only; no continuous numeric measurements.

Table_2.title_or_caption: Clinical features of p.S891A RET mutation carriers
Table_2.description: Provides per-case clinical data for p.S891A carriers (II-3, III-2, III-3, III-6, III-10, IV-2): gender, age at diagnosis, pre- and post-surgery basal serum calcitonin, ultrasound results, surgery type, histology, numbers of positive and resected lymph nodes, and pTNM stage.
Table_2.numeric_values_present: Ages (years), calcitonin levels (ng/L), tumor sizes (cm), counts of positive/resected lymph nodes, TNM categories.

Figure_1.title_or_caption: Pedigree of RET proto-oncogene mutation p.S891A in familial medullary thyroid carcinoma.
Figure_1.description: Pedigree diagram of four-generation FMTC family showing individuals with and without RET mutation, p.S891A carriers, MTC patients, proband, suspected mutation in deceased carrier, and individuals without samples.

Figure_2.title_or_caption: A heterozygous serine to alanine amino acid substitution within exon 15 of RET proto-oncogene, the missense c.2671T>G (p.S891A) mutation in proband.
Figure_2.description: Sequencing chromatogram demonstrating heterozygous c.2671T>G transition in RET exon 15.

Figure_3.title_or_caption: A variant c.833C>A(p.T278N) within exon 4 of RET proto-oncogene.
Figure_3.description: Sequencing chromatogram demonstrating c.833C>A substitution in RET exon 4.

discussion_notes: p.S891A is an intracellular RET mutation in the TK2 region that appears to function as an active monomer, without requiring dimerization and without further ligand-induced autophosphorylation or downstream signaling enhancement.
p.S891A is categorized among RET mutations with low transforming activity in vitro (alongside p.E768D, p.V804L, and p.A919P), in contrast to highly transforming mutations such as p.A883F and p.M918T.
The present pedigree represents, to the authors’ knowledge, the first report of a RET p.S891A mutation in an Asian (Chinese Han) familial MTC family.
Intracellular p.S891A mutations are rare overall, accounting for less than 5% of all families with MEN2-related MTC in previous series.

In a literature summary of 85 p.S891A carriers, MTC occurred in 72.9%, pheochromocytoma in 3.5%, hyperparathyroidism in 3.5%, and corneal nerve thickening in 3.5%, with a mean age of MTC diagnosis of 42.3 years.
Age-related penetrance of MTC in 36 p.S891A RET carriers was reported as 20.0% for ages 0–20 years, 71.4% for 21–40 years, 92.3% for 41–60 years, and 100% for over 60 years.

In this Chinese Han pedigree, the mean age of MTC onset among five of six affected individuals was 41.2 years (range 24–62), and 83.3% of p.S891A carriers exhibited MTC as the only clinical symptom without pheochromocytoma, hyperparathyroidism, corneal nerve thickening, or Hirschsprung disease.
Four of five MTC patients (80%) in this family presented with lymph node or bone metastases (two T1N1bM0, one T2N1bM0, and one T2N1bM1), which is consistent with prior clinical data on p.S891A.

The RET polymorphisms p.A45A, IVS4+48A>G, p.A432A, p.G691S, and p.L769L and the p.T278N variant have been associated with susceptibility and phenotypes such as C-cell hyperplasia, sporadic MTC, papillary thyroid carcinoma, Hirschsprung disease, MEN2A, and FMTC in other populations, but no phenotype associations were observed in this family.

The p.T278N variant has not previously been reported to play a role in MEN2-related MTC and may be benign or a single nucleotide polymorphism; its co-occurrence with p.S891A in one mildly affected carrier and presence alone in two unaffected individuals suggests a possible inhibitory modifier role, but further studies are required.

Early and normative surgery remains the most effective treatment for MTC, and systematic RET screening and presurgical calcitonin measurement allow earlier diagnosis and more appropriate initial management.

The 2009 ATA guidelines classify codon p.S891A as an ATA-A level mutation with lower transforming activity and a milder disease course, but still recommend total thyroidectomy with neck and level VI dissection in the presence of clinical lymph node metastases and prophylactic total thyroidectomy in childhood for at-risk carriers.
Despite advances, FMTC is frequently diagnosed only after lymph node metastasis or marked calcitonin elevation, and disease-free survival has not significantly improved over recent decades.

In this series, two patients undergoing non-normative initial surgeries experienced persistent elevated calcitonin and required multiple reoperations, underscoring the importance of appropriate initial management.
Even with normative surgery, as in patient III-3 with very high preoperative calcitonin, distant metastasis can occur, reflecting variable aggressiveness of MTC in p.S891A carriers.
Total thyroidectomy and modified bilateral lymph node excision can be curative in FMTC patients with cervical lymph node metastases when performed early and comprehensively.
For lower-risk mutations such as p.S891A, the necessity of prophylactic level VI compartment dissection in the absence of clinical or imaging evidence of lymph node metastasis remains uncertain.

ATA 2009 recommends prophylactic level VI lymph node dissection for MEN2A or FMTC patients with thyroid nodules ≥5 mm or basal calcitonin >40 ng/L, whereas other studies support individualized surgery and potential omission of lymph node dissection in carriers with basal calcitonin below 60–71.4 ng/L.
In this study, prophylactic level VI compartment dissection for patient IV-2 (preoperative calcitonin 12.60 ng/L, tumor 0.5 cm) is considered potentially controversial or unnecessary in retrospect.

Data from Elisei et al. support timing prophylactic total thyroidectomy in RET mutation carriers with negative basal calcitonin based on stimulated calcitonin positivity rather than genotype or age alone.
The refusal of prophylactic thyroidectomy by carrier III-6, despite elevated calcitonin, highlights the importance of patient education regarding risks and benefits to avoid delayed treatment.
Inter-individual differences in MTC progression suggest that genetic modifiers, including RET variants and tumor heterogeneity, may influence transforming activity and disease course.
The authors advocate individualized treatment of MEN2-related FMTC based on predictive RET screening and presurgical calcitonin levels.
They conclude that RET screening and presurgical calcitonin assessment are essential for earlier diagnosis and more normative initial treatment in FMTC patients with p.S891A mutation.

statistics.formal_tests: No hypothesis tests or confidence intervals reported.
